text: Elite athlete status is a partially heritable trait, as are the underpinning physiological, anthropometrical, and psychological traits that contribute to elite performance.
Genetic testing alone is currently insufficient to reliably predict future elite athlete status and should be combined with other identification tools for accurate talent assessment.
Total genotype scores composed of a high number of performance-enhancing polymorphisms are likely one of the best strategies for using genetic information to identify sport talent.
Genetic information may hold utility in guiding individualized nutritional recommendations for elite athletes.
Genetic information may hold utility in guiding individualized training recommendations for athletes.
Genetic information may assist in the prevention of sport-related injury by identifying individual predispositions.
